content: My family situation has been really complicated lately. My parents are divorced and both remarried, so holidays are always a juggling act.
That does sound complicated. How do you typically manage the holiday scheduling?
I usually split Thanksgiving with my mom and her husband in Boston, and Christmas with my dad and his wife in Salt Lake City where I just bought a house.
Congratulations on the new house! How's the renovation going?
Thanks! It's been a process. I'm doing most of the work myself since I love 3D printing and building things. The kitchen is almost done, just need to install the new cabinets.
That's impressive! Are you enjoying the DIY aspect of homeownership?
I am, though it's time-consuming. Between that and my job as a financial analyst at Goldman Sachs, I barely have time for my other hobbies like mountain biking and home brewing beer.
Sounds like you've got a lot on your plate. How do your parents feel about you being so busy with the house and work?

They're proud but worried I'm overextending myself. My mom especially keeps telling me to slow down. She's always been the nurturing type, even though she's remarried now to this great guy named David.
It's nice that you have that support system. Do you have siblings who help with the family dynamics?
I have a younger brother, Marcus, who lives in Portland. He's a surf instructor and much more laid-back than me. We're pretty different but close.
That's great that you're close despite being different. How does he handle the holiday scheduling?
He usually just goes with the flow and visits whichever parent we're not with. Last year he joined me in Salt Lake City for Christmas, which was nice.
That sounds like a good solution. Do you ever get to take vacations just for yourself, maybe to indulge in your love of sci-fi novels or surfing?
I try to take a week off each summer to go to Comic-Con in San Diego. Last year I finally got to meet my favorite sci-fi author, which was a dream come true.
